name: Willie Dixon
abstract: William James Dixon (July 1, 1915 – January 29, 1992) was an American blues musician, vocalist, songwriter, arranger and record producer. He was proficient in playing both the upright bass and the guitar, and sang with a distinctive voice, but he is perhaps best known as one of the most prolific songwriters of his time.

concerts.label: The Highlander Folk School Benefit Concert

works_created.t: I Just Want to Make Love to You

about_quotes.speaker: George "Happy" Schilling
about_quotes.evidence: I had a fellow by the name of Dixon playing piano. Do you know Willie Dixon, the piano man? He was very good. Very good man. But he left me at, and we left the yard club...

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: I got a lot of CDs that Hal Ashby and myself were on with Willie Dixon.
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I was twenty-two, twenty three. And the four o’clock show was Willie Dixon. I said, “Well, I ain’t never heard Willie Dixon, let me go.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sippie Wallace
about_quotes.evidence: So Willie asked me would I go to London... So then they had Willie to come and interview me from Chicago... He say, 'Now, I want some'
